categories.label: Plastic
facts: Plastic bottles take up to 450 years to decompose.
Only 9% of all plastic ever produced has been recycled.
Rinse plastic containers before recycling them.

categories.label: Organic
facts: Organic waste makes up about 50% of household trash.
Composting organic waste reduces methane emissions.
Food scraps and garden waste can become rich compost in 2–3 months.

categories.label: Paper
facts: Recycling one ton of paper saves 17 trees.
Paper can be recycled up to 7 times before fibers break down.
Wet or greasy paper (like pizza boxes) cannot be recycled.

categories.label: Glass
facts: Glass can be recycled indefinitely without losing quality.
Recycling glass saves 30% of the energy needed to make new glass.
Different colored glass (clear, green, brown) should be sorted separately.

categories.label: Hazardous
facts: Batteries contain toxic metals like mercury, lead, and cadmium.
Never throw electronics in regular trash — use e-waste drop-offs.
Used motor oil can contaminate millions of liters of groundwater.
